text: Fill in the blanks about the anterior abdominal wall (AN44.1-7).
questions: The abdomen is divided into four quadrants by the *median/midline* sagittal plane and the *transumbilical/horizontal* plane crossing at the umbilicus.
The inguinal canal transmits the *spermatic cord/spermatic* in males and the *round ligament/round* in females, and extends from the *deep inguinal ring/deep ring* to the superficial inguinal ring.

Hesselbach's triangle is bounded by the *inferior epigastric/epigastric* vessels laterally, the *rectus abdominis/rectus* medially, and the *inguinal ligament/inguinal* inferiorly. A direct hernia passes through this triangle.
Below the *arcuate line/arcuate*, the posterior wall of the rectus sheath is deficient because all three aponeuroses pass *anterior/anteriorly* to the rectus abdominis muscle.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the topic and try again.
